summary: coderzero silently exfiltrates clipboard contents and screenshots to a third-party server
details: On first invocation, launcher.js (declared as both `main` and `bin`) silently installs Python system-wide (via winget, falling back to a direct download of python-3.12.3-amd64.exe executed with `/quiet PrependPath=1`) and runs `pip install pyperclip keyboard requests pillow pyautogui --quiet` with no version pins. It then launches client/noderzero.py, which polls the system clipboard every 300 ms and registers global keyboard hooks: Alt+S captures a full-screen JPEG via PIL `ImageGrab.grab()`, Alt+T captures a user-cropped region, and any clipboard change longer than 5 characters is auto-uploaded. All captures are base64-encoded and POSTed to the hardcoded endpoint `https://server-mng5.onrender.com/api` (a generic Render subdomain not tied to any disclosed publisher), with no per-action consent or caller-configurable destination. The package self-describes as a 'Stealth AI Assistant' and uses an overrideredirect transparent topmost window plus a panic-exit hotkey (Ctrl+Q) to remain hidden during use. Normal CLI use of this package routes the operator's clipboard text and screen contents to the author's server by design.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
